properties.remark: WATER RESCUES REPORTED AND WATER WAS REPORTED INSIDE OF THE FIRE DEPARTMENT IN VAN BUREN.
SEVERAL BOATS AND TRAILERS WERE FLIPPED OVER AT A MARINE SHOP. GLASS DOORS WERE BLOWN OUT OF THE SHOP BUILDING. SEVERAL TREES WERE DOWN IN THE DONIPHAN AREA AS WELL AS A CARPORT THAT WAS BLOWN OVER.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND SCATTERED DAMAGE CAUSED BY STRAIGHT-LINE THUNDERSTORM WINDS. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 75 MPH. THE EVENT BEGAN AT 8:08 AM CST PARALLELING MISSOURI HIGHWAY 142 FROM 0.5 MILE NORTH OF DONIPHAN, MO TO 1 MILE SOUTH OF DONIPHAN, MO. THE ESTIMATED PATH LENGTH WAS 1.5 MILES, AND THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 1.5 MILES. SCATTERED TREE DAMAGE ACROSS THE DONIPHAN COMMUNITY, INCLUDING A LARGE PINE THAT WAS UPROOTED. A CARPORT WAS TURNED AND DAMAGED AND SEVERAL METAL, FLAT-BOTTOM FISHING BOATS BOATS WERE OVERTURNED AT A BOAT SHOP. A GLASS DOOR WAS SHATTERED AT THE BOAT SHOP. A POWER POLE WAS SNAPPED NEAR A HOUSE THAT SUSTAINED LIGHT ROOF AND FENCE DAMAGE.
1 INCH TO 1.5 INCH HAIL REPORTED IN UNIONTOWN.
SPOTTER REPORTED AT LEAST AN INCH DEPTH OF WATER FLOWING OVER IL-130 IN 8 DIFFERENT AREAS BETWEEN BONE GAP AND PARKERSBURG.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE IN DEXTER, MO. ESTIMATED PEAK WINDS WERE 90 MPH. THE DAMAGE STARTED ON THE SOUTHWEST SIDE OF DEXTER AND ENDED ON THE NORTHEAST SIDE OF DEXTER. THE EVENT BEGAN AT 9:00 A.M. CST AND ENDED AT 9:05 A.M. CST. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 3.1 MILES AND HAD A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 0.75 MILES. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED. SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED SHINGLE LOSS OR PARTIAL ROOF DAMAGE. A MOVING TRAIN CARRYING EMPTY AND FULL METAL SHIPPING CONTAINERS WAS DERAILED. SEVERAL STORAGE SHEDS AT BUSY BEES LUMBAR WERE DAMAGED. A COUPLE BUSINESSES HAD LARGE ADVERTISING SIGNS BLOWN DOWN.

AN NWS DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND SPORADIC STRAIGHT-LINE WIND DAMAGE IN THE SOUTHERN PART OF CAPE GIRARDEAU COUNTY, BEGINNING TWO MILES SOUTH OF WHITEWATER, MO, AND ENDING AT CAPE GIRARDEAU, MO. PEAK ESTIMATED WINDS WERE 80 MPH. THE EVENT BEGAN AT 9:00 A.M. CST AND ENDED AT 9:20 A.M. CST. THE PATH LENGTH WAS 15 MILES AND HAD A MAXIMUM WIDTH OF 1.5 MILES. ONE OPEN STORAGE BARN WAS DESTROYED, AND OTHER NEARBY BARNS SUSTAINED ROOF DAMAGE. A FORMER SCHOOL BUILDING RECEIVED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. A FEW HOMES SUSTAINED SHINGLE AND ROOF DAMAGE. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED.
A TRAIN WAS DERAILED DUE TO THUNDERSTORM WINDS.
TREE SNAPPED AT THE BASE AND FELL ON A HOUSE.
0.80 INCHES OF RAIN REPORTED FROM THE STORM AND WATER BEGINNING TO FLOW OVER A NEIGHBORHOOD ROAD.
ROOF AND TREE DAMAGE REPORTED.
CAMPERS OVERTURNED AT LARRYS TRAILER SALES.
60 KT WIND GUST REPORTED WITH TREE LIMBS DOWN IN THE AREA.
A TRAIN STRUCK A FALLEN TREE OVER THE RAILROAD TRACKS IN VILLA RIDGE IL.
DELAYED REPORT. FRONT SECTION OF AN OLD SCHOOL BUILDING WAS KNOCKED DOWN BY STRAIGHT-LINE THUNDERSTORM WINDS. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
TWO BARNS DESTROYED AND DOWNED POWER LINES REPORTED ALONG COUNTY ROAD 208.
WIND GUST AT NATIONAL WEATHER SERVICE OFFICE.
POWER LINES DOWN AT THE INTERSECTION OF SHAFFER ROAD AND NOBLE ROAD. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
POWER LINES DOWN NEAR THE INTERSECTION OF DR. MLK JR. DRIVE AND N. 11TH ST. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
WATER COVERING LOW LYING PORTIONS OF E VIENNA STREET NEAR MCDONALDS.

A NWS DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND TORNADO DAMAGE WITH A PEAK INTENSITY RATING OF EF-1. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 1.5 MILES WEST-SOUTHWEST OF OAKLAND CITY, IN AT 1112 AM CST AND LIFTED 1.0 MILES EAST-NORTHEAST OF OAKLAND CITY, IN BY 1116 AM CST. MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WIND SPEED WAS 110 MPH. THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 100 YARDS. THE PATH LENGTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 2.6 MILES. A LARGE METAL STORAGE BUILDING LOST MOST OF ITS ROOF AT GOLF COURSE. SEVERAL APARTMENT BUILDINGS WITH SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE WITH SHINGLES REMOVED. SELF STORAGE BUILDING NEAR APARTMENTS HAD PARTIAL ROOF DAMAGE. ADDITIONAL HOMES HAD MINOR SHINGLE DAMAGE. SEVERAL LARGE TREES SNAPPED AND KNOCKED DOWN.
ROOFS OFF OF SEVERAL BUSINESS BUILDINGS, POWER POLES SNAPPED, SIGNIFICANT DAMAGE TO A STORAGE FACILITY OFF OF SR-57. POWER OUTAGES REPORTED ACROSS THE AREA.

A NWS DAMAGE SURVEY FOUND TORNADO DAMAGE WITH A PEAK INTENSITY RATING OF EF-1. THE TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 3.6 NORTHWEST OF ROBARDS, KY AT 1157 AM CST AND LIFTED 3.6 NORTH-NORTHWEST OF ROBARDS, KY AT 1159 AM CST. THE MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WIND WAS 100 MPH. THE MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH WAS 30 YARDS. THE PATH LENGTH OF THE TORNADO WAS 1.2 MILES. A METAL OUTBUILDING COMPLETELY LOST ITS ROOF WITH WALLS PARTIALLY COLLAPSED. ONE LARGE TREE TRUNK SNAPPED. ROOFING MATERIAL LOFTED QUITE A DISTANCE INTO TREES. OTHER MINOR TREE DAMAGE.

WATER RESCUE DUE TO VEHICLE STALLED IN FLOOD WATER NORTH OF MURRAY STATE UNIVERSITY ON N. 16TH STREET.
HIGH WATER IS THREATENING A CHURCH AND A COUPLE OF HOMES IN ELKTON. WATER COVERS PORTIONS OF STATE ROADS 181, 848, AND 475, AS WELL AS SEVERAL COUNTY ROADS ACROSS THE COUNTY.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 9.5 MILES SW OF MORGANFIELD AT APPROX. 1010 AM AND LIFTED 2.5 MILES NNW OF MORGANFIELD AT APPROX. 1022 AM CDT. TORNADO BLEW OVER A SEMI, INJURING THE DRIVER NEAR HWY 109-HART HUCKEBY RD. BLEW A CAR AND TRAILER UPSIDE DOWN. DE-ROOFED A FARM BLDG. OFF OF HWY 948. THE TORNADO UPROOTED A COUPLE TREES AND BROKE SEVERAL TREE LIMBS ALONG AND WEST OF MCCRACKEN COUNTY ROAD TO THE NORTHEAST OF HICKORY. A PICTURE OF THE TORNADO WAS TAKEN FROM THE WEST SIDE OF HIGHWAY 45 IN HICKORY.

DELAYED REPORT. TELEVISION ANTENNA TOWER NEAR A HOME WAS KNOCKED OVER. REPORTED SUBMITTED VIA FACEBOOK. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.

A BRIEF EF-0 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS OF 70 MPH TOUCHED DOWN 1.1 MILES NORTHEAST OF SLAUGHTERS, KY. DAMAGE WAS LIMITED TO DISTURBED STUBBLE IN AGRICULTURAL FIELDS ALONG US 41 NORTH OF SLAUGHTERS. THE TORNADO LIFTED TWO MINUTES LATER ABOUT 3.25 MILES NE OF SLAUGHTERS. THE PATH LENGTH WAS APPROXIMATELY 2.3 MILES, AND THE MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 50 YARDS. THE TORNADO WAS CAPTURED ON VIDEO NEAR ITS BEGINNING.
PORTIONS OF A POLE BARN WERE BLOWN OVER 100 YARDS NORTHEAST FROM THE ORIGINAL LOCATION ON 15TH STREET JUST EAST OF URBIAN RD.

*** 1 INJ *** A NWS STORM DAMAGE SURVEY REVEALED DAMAGE FROM AN EF2 TORNADO THAT STARTED ALONG CROOKED CREEK IN WESTERN BOLLINGER COUNTY. SEVERAL SMALL SHEDS WERE MOVED OR DAMAGED ALONG THE PATH. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED OR SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH AS WELL. A MAN RECEIVED MINOR SCRATCHES ON HIS BACK WHEN THE TORNADO DEMOLISHED HIS MOBILE HOME ON COUNTY ROAD 344. THE TORNADO ENDED ALONG COUNTY ROAD 342. PATH LENGTH 9.6 MILES. MAXIMUM WIDTH WAS 250 YARDS.
A ROOF WAS TORN OFF OF A BARN, AND A COUPLE OF PORTABLE BUILDINGS WERE TOSSED. TOPS OF SEVERAL TREES REPORTED AS TWISTED.
A MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED APPROXIMATELY 150 YARDS FROM ITS ORIGINAL LOCATION.
A SINGLE WIDE MOBILE HOME WAS ROLLED APPROXIMATELY 150 YARDS FROM ITS ORIGINAL LOCATION. NUMEROUS TREES DOWN AND DOWN POWER POLES ALSO REPORTED.
SPOTTER REPORTED VIA SCANNER TRAFFIC MULTIPLE TREES DOWN AROUND THE BEECHMONT, DRAKESBORO AREAS, ONE MOTOR VEHICLE ACCIDENT DUE TO THE DEBRIS. POWER LINE DAMAGE AS WELL.
TIN PEELED BACK ON 2 BARNS. SCATTERED TREES DOWN. ONE OUTBUILDING LOST TIN ROOF.
TREES DOWN ALONG LACKEY ROAD BLOWN TO THE NORTH. RATED EF-0 WITH 70 MPH WINDS.

AN NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM PEAK WINDS OF 85 MPH BEGAN ABOUT 0.6 MILES SSW OF LACENTER, KY, ALONG KY HIGHWAY 802. THE TORNADO PATH WAS 0.5 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 85 YARDS. SEVERAL TREES LOST LARGE LIMBS, AND A FEW LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH. A 20 BY 25 FOOT SECTION OF ROOF ON A HOME ON KENTUCKY HIGHWAY 358 WAS LIFTED AND THROWN ABOUT 150 FEET. A NEARBY STORAGE BUILDING HAD ITS DOORS BLOWN IN, AND THE BASE OF AN EXTERIOR WALL WAS SHIFTED BY A COUPLE FEET. A FEW OTHER HOMES IN THE VICINITY RECEIVED MINOR SHINGLE DAMAGE. THE TORNADO LIFTED SHORTLY AFTER CROSSING HIGHWAY 358, ABOUT 0.4 MILES SW OF LACENTER.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 1 SSW LA CENTER. AN NWS STORM SURVEY FOUND AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM PEAK WINDS OF 85 MPH BEGAN ABOUT 0.6 MILES SSW OF LACENTER, KY, ALONG KY HIGHWAY 802. THE TORNADO PATH WAS 0.5 MILES WITH A MAXIMUM PATH WIDTH OF 85 YARDS. SEVERAL TREES LOST LARGE LIMBS, AND A FEW LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED ALONG THE PATH. A 20 BY 25 FOOT SECTION OF ROOF ON A HOME ON KENTUCKY HIGHWAY 358 WAS LIFTED AND THROWN ABOUT 150 FEET. A NEARBY STORAGE BUILDING HAD ITS DOORS BLOWN IN, AND THE BASE OF AN EXTERIOR WALL WAS SHIFTED BY A COUPLE FEET. A FEW OTHER HOMES IN THE VICINITY RECEIVED MINOR SHINGLE DAMAGE. THE TORNADO LIFTED SHORTLY AFTER CROSSING HIGHWAY 358, ABOUT 0.4 MILES SE OF LACENTER.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TSTM WND DMG REPORT FROM ST. JOSEPH. NWS DAMAGE SURVEY DETERMINED MICROBURST WITH PEAK WINDS NEAR 90 MPH EXTENDED FROM ST JOSEPH TO WEST LOUISVILLE. AN EMBEDDED GUSTNADO OCCURRED ONE HALF MILE NORTH OF ST JOSEPH, WHERE A GARAGE WALL WITH HURRICANE STRAPS WAS PUSHED INWARD TO THE EAST. AN ADJACENT STORAGE TRAILER WEIGHING AT LEAST 2000 POUNDS WAS BLOWN 30 FEET TO THE NORTH. ELSEWHERE, SCATTERED TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED, INCLUDING OVER 100 TREES AT A LARGE CAMPGROUND AT WEST LOUISVILLE. SEVERAL BARNS WERE DAMAGED OR DESTROYED.

THE TORNADO WAS MINOR ROOF DAMAGE TO AT LEAST EIGHT HOMES, PRIMARILY FROM FALLING TREES. ONE HOME HAD A BROKEN WINDOW. ONE BARN WAS LEVELED. SEVERAL POWER LINES WERE DOWNED, MAINLY FROM FALLING TREES. AT LEAST TWO HUNDRED TREES WERE UPROOTED, SNAPPED OR HAD BROKEN LIMBS. THE TORNADO TRAVELED MAINLY ALONG AND NORTH OF KINGS CHAPEL ROAD INITIALLY, THEN TRAVELED ALONG COOL SPRING CAVE ROAD BEFORE CROSSING SOUTH MONTGOMERY ROAD, NORTH OF CALEDONA.
HIGHWAY 120 AND HIGHWAY 630 HAVE WATER OVER THEM AND THEY ARE CLOSED.
MEASURED AT WKDZ RADIO. TIME ESTIMATED.

THIS CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 1.4 MILES NORTHEAST OF HERNDON. THE PATH LENGTH WAS ADJUSTED TO 7.6 MILES. THE TORNADO APPEARED TO BEGIN IN A CORNFIELD ALONG HWY 164 NORTH OF PEEDEE. IT THEN CONTINUED EAST/SOUTHEAST WHERE A BARN COLLAPSED. IT DAMAGED AROUND A DOZEN HOMES, MAINLY TO SOFFITS AND ROOFING. NUMEROUS LARGE TREES WERE SNAPPED AND UPROOTED ALONG THE PATH. THE TORNADO APPEARS TO HAVE LIFTED JUST WEST OF THE INTERSTATE 24/PENNYRILE PARKWAY INTERCHANGE.

EF1 TORNADO APPEARED TO START ALONG PIERCE LN NEAR BOYD ROAD AND TRAVELED ALONG PIERCE LN FOR ALMOST A MILE. IT DESTROYED 3 EMPTY GRAIN BINS AND MOVED INTO A FIELD. MOST OF THE DAMAGE WAS ON THE SOUTH FLANK OF THE TORNADO. MANY LARGE, OLD TREES WERE SNAPPED OR UPROOTED. TOWARDS THE END OF THE PATH A HOUSE LOST HALF OF ITS ROOF. PATH LENGTH ABOUT 1 MILE LONG.

EF 1 TORNADO MOVED ALONG STRIPED BRIDGE RD SOUTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF WOOSLEY RD. TORNADO DID MAINLY SOFFIT AND ROOF DAMAGE TO 8-10 HOMES ALONG STRIPED BRIDGE RD. ONE SMALL GUEST HOUSE WAS DESTROYED. PATH LENGTH WAS .6 MILES.

AN EF-1 TORNADO WITH MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS OF 100 MPH DAMAGED PART OF A MARINA AT MOORS RESORT ON KENTUCKY LAKE IN MARSHALL COUNTY, KY. A 50 YARD SECTION OF MARINA, ONE OF THREE SECTIONS THAT EXTENDED INTO KENTUCKY LAKE, WAS TWISTED AND OVERTURNED BY THE TORNADO. A BOAT THAT WAS DOCKED IN THIS PART OF THE MARINA WAS LIFTED ONTO THE OVERTURNED MARINA SECTION. THE ENTIRE MARINA STRUCTURE WAS SHIFTED SEVERAL FEET AND PULLED AWAY FROM THE SHORE, DISLODGING SEVERAL SPUD POLES THAT ANCHORED THE FLOATING MARINA STRUCTURE TO THE LAKE BOTTOM. ACROSS THE REST OF MARSHALL COUNTY, SCATTERED STRAIGHT-LINE DAMAGE INCLUDED BROKEN LARGE TREE LIMBS AND UPROOTED TREES.
